_ifi_metadata.essay_text:
​Fatherhood Essay​
​It​​was​​an​​intensely​​snowy​​day,​​so​​much​​that​​the​​school​​accepted​​if​​people​
​were​ ​not​ ​going​ ​to​ ​show​ ​up​ ​that​ ​day​ ​and​ ​give​ ​the​ ​students​ ​a​ ​three​ ​day​
​weekend.​ ​However​ ​I​ ​didn’t​ ​get​ ​that​ ​courtesy​ ​as​ ​I​​was​​already​​on​​my​​way.​
​When​​I​​arrived​​I​​soon​​realized​​that​​I​​was​​one​​of​​the​​only​​people​​there,​​aside​
​from​​a​​couple​​of​​younger​​students​​and​​one​​of​​my​​classmates,​​Kailynn.​​The​
​Browns,​ ​Mr.​ ​and​ ​Mrs.​ ​Brown​ ​took​ ​this​ ​as​ ​an​ ​opportunity​ ​to​ ​have​ ​a​​snow​
​day.​ ​Snow​ ​days​ ​only​ ​ever​ ​occurred​ ​once​ ​every​ ​blue​ ​moon​ ​and​ ​usually​
​consisted​​of​​no​​schoolwork​​at​​all.​​My​​face​​instantly​​brightened​​as​​I​​realized​
​that my efforts to get here were not just for a regular day.​
​First,​ ​the​ ​Browns​ ​started​ ​by​ ​ordering,​ ​the​ ​four​ ​of​ ​us​ ​some​ ​Chinese​ ​food​
​from​​down​​the​​street​​and​​getting​​it​​delivered​​to​​the​​school,​​seeing​​as​​since​
​it​ ​was​ ​the​ ​four​ ​of​ ​us​ ​it​ ​was​ ​affordable​ ​for​ ​the​ ​day.​ ​While​ ​we​ ​waited,​ ​Mr.​
​Brown​​had​​went​​to​​put​​on​​a​​variety​​of​​Christmas​​movies​​for​​us​​to​​watch.​​He​
​tracked​ ​his​ ​computer​ ​cursor​ ​all​ ​the​ ​way​ ​to​ ​The​ ​Nightmare​ ​Before​
​Christmas​.​ ​Mr.​ ​Brown​ ​put​ ​on​ ​the​ ​movie​ ​as​ ​the​ ​four​ ​of​ ​us​ ​set​ ​up​ ​chairs.​
​About​​halfway​​through​​the​​movie,​​the​​Chinese​​food​​had​​arrived​​for​​all​​of​
​us to eat.​
​During​​the​​moment,​​I​​felt​​a​​sense​​of​​gratitude​​and​​familial​​bond​​towards​
​Mr.​ ​Brown.​ ​Simply​ ​the​ ​fact​ ​that​ ​he​ ​was​ ​kine​ ​enough​ ​to​ ​put​ ​aside​ ​his​
​teaching​ ​for​ ​a​ ​day​​for​​us​​to​​have​​a​​day​​of​​fun.​​We​​continued​​to​​eat​​and​
​then​ ​walked​ ​down​ ​to​ ​the​ ​gymnasium​ ​so​ ​that​ ​we​ ​could​ ​play​ ​some​
​basketball.​​In​​the​​heat​​of​​the​​moment​​I​​was​​just​​so​​glad​​to​​have​​Mr.​​Brown​
​as​​a​​teacher​​but​​also​​to​​have​​him​​around​​as​​a​​father​​figure​​as​​well.​​Finally​
​to​​end​​off​​the​​day​​we​​walked​​back​​to​​the​​original​​classroom​​to​​have​​some​
​time​​for​​quiet​​and​​peace.​​During​​the​​whole​​day​​I​​knew​​that​​not​​only​​was​​Mr.​
​Brown a father figure, it was like having him as a secondary father as well.​
